mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications requiring efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data, such as AI applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for deployment and the complexity of managing distributed systems.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for Milvus.

steps.title: Install Milvus CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Milvus to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

```

> This CRD is necessary for managing Milvus clusters.

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Milvus in the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

> Replace '2.5.0' with the desired version.

steps.title: Create a Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Milvus cluster by applying a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1alpha1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a basic Milvus cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1alpha1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Milvus installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Milvus pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> You should see all Milvus pods in the Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss unless backups are made.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> This command removes the Milvus release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the Milvus installation to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.6.0

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Drain the nodes before upgrading.
> Replace '2.6.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MilvusCluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus cluster resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Milvus cluster resource definition using the provided YAML.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be running or accessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Milvus service is running and check the service endpoints.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for Milvus.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Milvus deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector index types and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated old index types in favor of new implementations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.4, ensure to update your index configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus pods running with the output similar to: 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' followed by the pod names and their statuses.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1alpha1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
